title: State of the Planet
description: News from the Columbia Climate School

items.title: New Research Indicates That in the Future, Trees May Store Less Carbon Than Expected
items.description: Even as trees photosynthesize late into the year, their growth stops by mid-summer, which impacts their carbon uptake.

items.title: What I’ve Learned From ‘The End of Poverty,’ 20 Years Later
items.description: The author reflects on Jeffrey Sachs, the U.N., and the need to redesign global institutions for a world shaped by climate change, poverty and geopolitical strain.

items.title: How Do Glacial Ecosystems Respond to Climate Change?
items.description: Microbiologist Arwyn Edwards discusses the complex role of microbial life in glacial ecosystems and the impact of climate change on his field.

items.title: You Asked: What Exactly Is a ‘Super’ El Niño?
items.description: Columbia Climate School experts explain what a strong El Niño could mean for the planet this year.

items.title: Geologist Folarin Kolawole Receives 2026 National Geographic Wayfinder Award
items.description: Folarin Kolawole is one of 15 recipients of this year's National Geographic Society’s Wayfinder Award, which recognizes people whose work is expanding our understanding of the planet and helping to protect its future.

items.title: How ‘Undone Science’ Shaped Chile’s Glacier Protection Battle
items.description: A new study examines how "undone science" has shaped the conflict between mining and glacier conservation in Chile over the past two decades.

items.title: World Oceans Day Reimagines Our Relationship With the Water Around Us
items.description: In honor of this annual U.N. event, we are highlighting our coverage of ocean research and education initiatives at the Columbia Climate School and Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory.

items.title: Raising AI for a Just Climate Future
items.description: Raising a responsible child and building responsible AI both require a deep awareness of what we are training them to notice and value.

items.title: Climate Finance Students Win Private Equity Case Competition
items.description: For the first time, a team from the inaugural M.S. in Climate Finance program participated in the prestigious Columbia Business School and KKR Private Equity Case Competition.

items.title: Regenerative Travel: Students Field Test What’s Next in Climate-Conscious Tourism
items.description: The Wild Tomorrow capstone workshop and Cuttyhunk Practicum are two examples of Climate School initiatives that focus on climate-conscious travel.
